details: Idempotent MOA seed executed with honest demo, placeholder, migration, QA, XR, XRetail, XLearning, XGaming, AI, analytics, accessibility, and admin records.

*SCAVerse public platform overview revamp*
• Rebuilt Platform Overview as a mass-market landing page.
• Added clear public actions: Explore Museums, Buy Tickets, Experience Virtual Museums, Become a Museum Partner.
• Added commercial positioning for ticket conversion, tenant isolation, mobile immersion, commerce scaling, analytics, and VR/3D readiness.
• Added an on-demand InvokeLLM museum launch brief generator.

*Bug tracker impact*
• Improved commercial readiness and public clarity.
• No existing proof-gate bug is fully resolved yet: route crawl, tenant mutation QA, mobile QA, accessibility QA, and build/typecheck evidence still need verification.

*SCAVerse isolation foundation update*
• Added architecture-only isolation contract.
• Defined tenant-scoped vs platform-scoped entity boundaries.
• Wired access checks through the isolation rules.
• Added deterministic tenant context resolution.
• Added isolation foundation documentation.

*Bug tracker impact*
• Partially addressed: tenant mutation isolation architecture gap.
• Still not fully resolved: mutation QA proof, full route crawl, mobile QA, accessibility QA, and build/typecheck evidence.

_No bug tracker item has been marked fully resolved yet._

*SCAVerse app update + bug tracker check*
• Completed the canonical SCAVerse architecture standardization pass.
• Updated admin route registry, blueprint source, validation notes, and the architecture reference document.

*Bug tracker check*
• Partially addressed: route integrity evidence and admin/public synchronization structure.
• Not fully resolved yet: full route crawl proof, CTA determinism proof, tenant mutation isolation proof, mobile QA, accessibility QA, and build/typecheck evidence.

_No bug tracker item has been marked fully resolved until verification evidence is completed._

*SCAVerse operational status*
Slack operational status updates are now connected to #museum-alerts using the shared Slack account.
